---
title: "GTPCNZ"
subtitle: "Primary care funding architecture model and dashboard"
---

GTPCNZ is the public reporting layer for a source-informed model scaffold about primary care funding in Aotearoa New Zealand.

## Start here

If you are new to the project, read these in order:

1. [Public report](reports/primary_care_architecture.qmd)
2. [Dashboard guide](docs/REPORTS-AND-DASHBOARD.md)
3. [Model card](docs/calibration/model-card-v1.7.2.md)
4. [Claim boundaries](docs/launch/claim-boundaries-v1.7.2.md)
5. [Evidence tracker](docs/public-site/evidence-tracker-public-v1.8.1.md)
6. [Calibration readiness](docs/public-site/calibration-readiness-page-v1.8.1.md)

## Current status

This is a **source-informed, parameterised policy model scaffold**.

> This is a source-informed parameterised scaffold and educational explainer. It is not a real-data calibrated forecast and should not be used to claim precise fiscal savings, hospital-demand reductions, workforce effects, or implementation impacts.

## Current thesis

The thesis is narrow. Keep capitation for continuity, enrolment, baseline viability and population responsibility. Add an uncapped, scheduled, rules-based fee-for-service stream for eligible primary medical activity, with item rules, provider scope, clinical governance, documentation, audit, co-payment protections and place-based accountability.

Short version:

> Uncapped does not mean uncontrolled.

## Public pages

- GitHub Pages: this site.
- Streamlit: [interactive explainer and scenario dashboard](https://gtpcnz.streamlit.app/).
- Repository: source code, outputs, model card, claim boundaries and tests.

## Local commands

Render the website:

```bash
quarto render
```

Run the dashboard:

```bash
streamlit run streamlit_app.py
```
